title: Snack: ½ cup plain yogurt with 1 tbsp peanut butter and 1 tsp jam [Coach: Well-timed snack — at 92 and trending down, the peanut butter and yogurt protein-fat combo will help arrest the slide. The small jam gives a quick carb lift while the PB and yogurt sustain it steadily. Good instinct to eat now.] (BG: 92 mg/dL FortyFiveDown) (Protein: 10g) (Pred: 115-125 mg/dL @ 7:07 PM)

Lunch: Half Reuben sandwich (corned beef, sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, Thousand Island dressing on toasted rye/sourdough), side salad of mixed greens, pickled red onion, and pumpkin seeds [Coach: Smart call eating only half — the corned beef has no carbs and good protein, sourdough fermentation slows digestion vs white bread, and the salad adds fiber and healthy fats. A well-managed lunch especially starting at 141.] (BG: 141 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 16g) (Pred: 158-168 mg/dL @ 2:34 PM)

Breakfast: Baguette with Brie cheese [Coach: Baguette with Brie is a classic — the Brie's fat slows the refined flour carbs and adds good protein. At 38g carbs the predicted peak is on the higher side starting at 136; a post-breakfast walk would help bring it back down nicely.] (BG: 136 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 14g) (Pred: 208-218 mg/dL @ 11:39 AM)

Dinner: Leftover homemade pappardelle Bolognese with shredded cheddar, braised pork rib, and dill pickle slices [Coach: Leftovers done right — the homemade pasta carries familiar carbs but ~44g of protein from the pork rib and Bolognese plus the fat from the cheese will slow absorption considerably. Pickles add a probiotic touch with virtually no sugar. Hearty and well-balanced.] (BG: 117 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 44g) (Pred: 164-174 mg/dL @ 7:59 PM)

Lunch: Chickpea bowl with wilted dark leafy greens, avocado slices, and shredded cheddar [Coach: A beautifully balanced bowl — chickpeas bring complex carbs with fiber and plant protein, dark greens add iron and antioxidants, avocado slows absorption with healthy fats, and cheese rounds out the protein. Very close to the ideal T2D plate. Starting at 99 with a predicted rise to only ~125 is excellent.] (BG: 99 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 17g) (Pred: 120-130 mg/dL @ 3:16 PM)

Breakfast: Flour tortilla with 1 egg and melted cheese (rolled), side of orange slices (~half large orange) [Coach: A solid breakfast — egg and cheese provide protein that slows the tortilla carb spike, and the orange adds vitamin C and natural fiber. At ~39g carbs with good protein coverage this is a well-balanced start to the day.] (BG: 116 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 18g) (Pred: 189-199 mg/dL @ 11:22 AM)

Dinner: Homemade artisan bread roll (~70g) [Cumulative Dinner: ~43g carbs total] [Coach: Homemade bread is a lovely addition — you control the ingredients and it pairs well with the protein-rich main. The fat and protein from the caprese and meat slow carb absorption considerably. At 43g total for the meal this is very manageable.] (BG: 100 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 6g) (Pred: 146-156 mg/dL @ 7:54 PM)

Dinner: Caprese salad (tomato slices with burrata/fresh mozzarella) and bone-in braised meat (short rib or lamb chop with dark glaze) [Coach: An exceptional T2D dinner — the caprese brings tomato fiber and protein from the mozzarella with almost no carbs, and the braised meat delivers ~32g protein with essentially zero starch. Starting at 101 with only 8g total carbs, the spike from this portion alone will be minimal.] (BG: 101 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 32g) (Pred: 110-120 mg/dL @ 7:54 PM)

Lunch: 2 slices red dragon fruit [Cumulative Lunch: ~50g carbs total] [Coach: Red dragon fruit is rich in antioxidants and a hydrating finish to the barley bowl. The 2-slice portion keeps it light on calories. Staying in the ×1.2 carb bracket gives a slightly higher predicted peak than crossing into the 51+g range would have — something to keep in mind with fruit additions.] (BG: 109 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 1g) (Pred: 159-169 mg/dL @ 2:06 PM)

Lunch: Barley bowl with stewed tomato and pepper veggies, 1 poached egg [Coach: Another barley bowl — consistently one of your best lunch choices. Barley's high fiber and the egg's protein make a solid base. Protein is a bit light at ~13g; a second egg or some sardines would bring you to the 20-30g target. Great repeat choice for blood sugar management.] (BG: 116 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 13g) (Pred: 148-158 mg/dL @ 2:06 PM)

Breakfast: Peanut butter toast with hemp hearts and low-sugar jam, cup of milk [Coach: Solid breakfast — peanut butter slows the toast carbs and hemp hearts add omega-3s and protein. Milk brings the total to ~20g protein — well done. Low-sugar jam is a smart choice. BG at 105 this morning confirms the orange last night didn't cause an overnight dip — good news.] (BG: 105 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 20g) (Pred: 177-187 mg/dL @ 10:36 AM)

Dessert: 1 medium orange, peeled and segmented [Coach: A lovely light evening snack — oranges are rich in vitamin C and fiber, and the Dessert intercept keeps the predicted rise modest. BG at 112 after your 30-minute walk is excellent. A very sensible way to satisfy a sweet craving at night.] (BG: 112 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 1g) (Pred: 116-126 mg/dL @ 10:06 PM)

Dinner: Homemade pappardelle (flour, eggs, olive oil) with Bolognese meat sauce and a piece of Brie [Coach: A classic Bolognese — the meat sauce brings solid protein and the fat content slows pasta digestion more than plain pasta would. The Brie adds more healthy fat and bumps protein to ~31g. Starting BG at 97 is ideal and the predicted peak is very manageable.] (BG: 97 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 31g) (Pred: 131-141 mg/dL @ 7:40 PM)

Lunch: Stir-fried noodles, sautéed leafy greens, pork shumai, scallop dumpling, papaya on the side (BG: 121 mg/dL Flat) (Pred: 157-167 mg/dL @ 2:53 PM) (Protein: 17g | Carbs: ~44g | Cals: ~323) [Coach: Better protein picture with the shumai and scallop dumpling — brings you to ~17g which is respectable. The dim sum wrappers add some carbs but not excessively. Papaya is gentler than watermelon on blood sugar. Good variety with the greens rounding it all out.]

Breakfast: Small plain quesadilla with ¼ avocado and natto [Coach: Great addition — natto is a T2D superfood: fermented soybeans bring probiotics, vitamin K2, and a solid protein boost bringing this to ~18g. Combined with avocado fats, this is a well-balanced plate. The quesadilla is still the main carb driver but the fats and protein will slow the rise nicely.] (BG: 113 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 18g) (Pred: 177-187 mg/dL @ 11:31 AM)

Dinner: Chocolate cake slice with whipped cream [Cumulative Dinner: ~75g carbs total] [Coach: After that outstanding barley-sardine-avocado dinner, a small chocolate treat is well earned. The whipped cream adds minimal extra sugar. Total dinner carbs at ~75g are manageable — your Trulicity and Metformin combo will help. Enjoy every bite.] (BG: 94 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 4g) (Pred: 154-164 mg/dL @ 8:36 PM)

Dinner: Barley bowl with stewed mixed vegetables, avocado, and sardines [Coach: This is an excellent T2D dinner — sardines bring omega-3s and solid protein, barley is a high-fiber whole grain, and avocado adds healthy fats that slow digestion. Stewed veggies round out the fiber. BG at 101 going in is perfect. One of the best-balanced dinners in your recent log.] (BG: 101 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 19g) (Pred: 146-156 mg/dL @ 8:36 PM)

Snack: Bowl of papaya chunks (~1.5 cups) [Coach: Your BG bounced back to 97 after that carb-heavy lunch — great recovery, the 2-mile walk made a real difference. Papaya is a gentle fruit choice with good fiber. The portion is a bit generous; keeping it to ~1 cup would keep the spike even lower. Still a solid snack.] (BG: 97 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 2g) (Pred: 125-135 mg/dL @ 7:42 PM)

Lunch: Dim sum plate — chicken feet, stuffed tofu roll, braised black mushroom [Cumulative Lunch: ~88g carbs total] [Coach: Good protein boost from this plate — chicken feet and stuffed tofu roll add substance. Cumulative carbs are at ~88g for the meal but Trulicity and Metformin together give you good glucose resilience. A walk after lunch would be a great idea.] (BG: 103 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 16g) (Pred: 149-159 mg/dL @ 3:21 PM)

Lunch: Tan tan noodles in sesame/peanut sauce with minced pork [Cumulative Lunch: ~77g carbs total] [Coach: Tan tan noodles on top of congee brings total carbs to ~77g — a heavy carb load for lunch. The sesame sauce adds healthy fat which helps slow absorption a bit. When eating Chinese, prioritize protein dishes and greens and keep starchy items small. Your morning walk is in your favor today.] (BG: 102 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 18g) (Pred: 141-151 mg/dL @ 3:21 PM)

Lunch: Congee with fish/seafood and green onion, sautéed leafy greens, Chinese tea [Coach: Congee has a high glycemic index — the broken-down rice digests quickly despite moderate carbs, so watch the peak timing. The leafy greens are excellent for fiber. Protein is light at ~10g for lunch; a tea egg, fish fillet, or tofu added would round this out nicely.] (BG: 96 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 10g) (Pred: 116-126 mg/dL @ 3:21 PM)

Breakfast: 2-egg omelette, slice of sourdough bread, orange wedges, small yogurt on the side [Coach: Nice balanced breakfast — eggs and yogurt together give you solid protein to anchor the meal. The bread and orange are moderate carb contributors but not excessive. Good variety and a strong start to the day.] (BG: 114 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 20g) (Pred: 178-188 mg/dL @ 11:22 AM)

Dessert: Papaya chunks and green grapes (~¾ cup papaya, 4 grapes) [Coach: Your BG is still at 155 from dinner, so this fruit adds to an elevated baseline — the 10:30 PM predicted peak reflects that. Papaya is gentler than grapes on blood sugar, so the pairing is okay. A short evening walk would help bring that dinner peak down before bed.] (BG: 155 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 1g) (Pred: 161-171 mg/dL @ 10:30 PM)

Dinner: Lentil stew with braised meat and potatoes in tomato broth, small baguette slice on the side [Coach: Lentil stew is one of the best T2D dinner choices — lentils are high in fiber and protein which slow carb absorption despite the carb count. The braised meat adds solid protein. The bread is the one item to watch — skipping it next time would shave ~15g carbs off an otherwise great meal.] (BG: 115 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 30g) (Pred: 163-173 mg/dL @ 7:58 PM)

Snack: Mango and cashews (½ oz each) [Coach: Very mindful portion — half an ounce of each keeps carbs very low and the cashews still provide enough fat to slow that small mango hit. Predicted rise is minimal. Great snack discipline.] (BG: 98 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 3g) (Pred: 113-123 mg/dL @ 6:36 PM)

Lunch: Barley bowl with sautéed mushrooms, sliced avocado, and fried egg, small orange on the side [Coach: Barley is a great whole-grain choice — high fiber and lower GI than rice means a gradual rise. Mushrooms add flavor with almost no carbs. Protein is a bit light at ~13g — adding a second egg or some chicken next time would get you closer to the 20-30g target. Good bowl.] (BG: 136 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 13g) (Pred: 166-176 mg/dL @ 2:32 PM)

Breakfast: Plain yogurt with papaya and hemp seeds + small baguette slice with peanut butter and reduced-sugar jam [Coach: Plain yogurt with papaya and hemp seeds is a great base — papaya is gentler than most fruits on blood sugar, and hemp seeds add protein and healthy fats. Peanut butter on the baguette slows carb absorption, and the reduced-sugar jam is a smart choice. Solid breakfast.] (BG: 103 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 20g) (Pred: 173-183 mg/dL @ 11:23 AM)

Lunch: Kale salad with 2 hard-boiled eggs, hemp hearts, 3 walnuts, goji berries, dressing [Coach: Beautiful lunch, Maria — kale with two eggs hits exactly what we want at midday: leafy greens plus 22g of lean protein. Hemp hearts and walnuts add great healthy fats and the goji berries bring a touch of natural sweetness without spiking carbs. Lovely balance.] (BG: 114 mg/dL Flat) (Protein: 22g) (Pred: 120-130 mg/dL @ 2:28 PM)
